grantTitle: Advanced manufacturing for key products (IA) (Made in Europe partnership)
programName: Horizon Europe - Cluster 4: Digital, Industry and Space
summary: This Innovation Action supports the development of advanced manufacturing technologies and machinery for key and high-performance products in Europe. The focus is on manufacturing excellence, increasing circularity through reuse of secondary raw materials and innovative materials, while reducing dependency on critical raw materials and imported components. Projects should achieve TRL 7 by completion, with emphasis on enhancing Europe's technological leadership and industrial resilience.
objectives: Make advanced manufacturing technology and machinery available in Europe for manufacturing key and high-performance products
Increase production circularity through reuse of secondary raw materials and incorporation of innovative advanced materials
Significantly increase resource efficiency in terms of materials and energy
Enhance circularity, productivity, competitiveness and resilience of European industry
Reduce dependency on critical raw materials and imported materials/parts
Develop technologies for manufacturing components with minimal use of critical raw materials
Ensure at least one funded proposal focuses on the automotive industry

requirements.description: Activities must start at TRL 5 and achieve TRL 7 by end of project
requirements.evidence: Technical development plan and TRL assessment methodology

requirements.description: Focus on advanced manufacturing technologies including innovative additive manufacturing, hybrid manufacturing, photonics, advanced joining, polymer composite manufacturing, surface treatment, lightweight materials manufacturing, and in-line testing
requirements.evidence: Technical description of manufacturing technologies to be developed

requirements.description: Target minimal use of critical raw materials or imported materials, with increased use of secondary raw materials, biobased materials or revalorised components
requirements.evidence: Materials strategy and circular economy approach

requirements.description: Where appropriate, incorporate innovative advanced materials (lightweight, functionalised, self-healing) to enhance performance and quality
requirements.evidence: Materials development and integration plan including SSbD-design steps and digital twins if applicable

requirements.description: Include a business case and exploitation strategy as outlined in the Destination introduction
requirements.evidence: Business case and exploitation strategy documentation (3 additional pages allowed in Part B)
Complete application through Submission System by deadline

requirements.description: Eligible countries as described in Annex B of Work Programme General Annexes (EU member states and associated countries)
Financial and operational capacity requirements as per Annex C
Battery production is explicitly excluded from this topic scope
International cooperation is encouraged, especially with Japan or Taiwan
requirements.evidence: Letters of commitment from international partners if applicable

evaluationCriteria.name: Excellence
evaluationCriteria.description: Quality and credibility of the proposed approach, technological innovation, advancement beyond state-of-art
keyIndicators: Innovation level and advancement of TRL from 5 to 7
Quality of materials strategy (secondary, biobased, innovative advanced materials)

evaluationCriteria.name: Impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Contribution to expected outcomes including technological leadership, circularity, resource efficiency, and European industrial competitiveness
keyIndicators: Enhanced European manufacturing competitiveness and strategic autonomy
Reduction in critical raw materials dependency
Quality of business case and exploitation strategy

evaluationCriteria.name: Quality and efficiency of implementation
evaluationCriteria.description: Quality of work plan, consortium composition, resource allocation and project management
keyIndicators: Appropriateness of consortium composition and expertise
Quality of work plan and resource allocation
Compliance with Made in Europe partnership implementation

categories.description: Direct costs for Innovation Actions under HORIZON-CL4-2026-01-MAT-PROD-01
rules: EU contribution follows standard Horizon Europe funding rates
Lump sum contributions may be authorized under specific conditions
Granting authority may object to transfer of ownership or exclusive licensing of results up to 4 years after action end

deliverables: Complete application submission through Funding & Tenders Portal

timeline.duration: As per Annex F timelines (typically 5 months)
As per Annex F timelines (typically 8 months from deadline)

targetBeneficiaries: Automotive industry (portfolio approach ensures at least one automotive-focused project)
eligibleCountries: Specific non-EU/non-Associated Countries with bilateral funding arrangements (see Horizon Europe Programme Guide)
International cooperation encouraged with Japan and Taiwan
additionalNotes: This topic implements the co-programmed European Partnership 'Made in Europe'
Portfolio approach ensures at least one automotive industry-focused proposal is funded (provided it meets all thresholds)
Battery production is explicitly excluded and addressed in separate topic HORIZON-CL5-2026-05-D2-03
Proposal page limit in Part B exceptionally extended by 3 pages to accommodate business case and exploitation strategy
International cooperation particularly encouraged with Japan and Taiwan
Development of advanced materials should not be the main focus, but adaptation steps for manufacturing applications should be included
Proposals should include digital twins for materials and SSbD-design steps where appropriate
Grants awarded not only by ranking but also to ensure portfolio balance (automotive industry coverage)
Results ownership transfers and exclusive licensing subject to granting authority objection rights for up to 4 years post-project
Type of Action: HORIZON Innovation Actions (IA)
Destination: Achieving technological leadership for Europe's open strategic autonomy in raw materials, chemicals and innovative materials (2026-27)
